cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down this scenario. We have a basketball game where Louie and Claire have a bet with their friends. They win $100 if either Louie makes a 3-point shot or Claire makes a 3-point shot. Claire makes a 3-point shot right at the beginning of the game. Then, for the rest of the game, neither Louie nor Claire can make another shot. Then, at the end of the game, Louie makes a 3-point shot right at the buzzer. They win the $100 and split it. The question is asking us to think about what could have been different, and specifically which event do we imagine happening differently: Claire making the 3-point shot at the beginning or Louie making the 3-point shot at the buzzer. Then the question is whether Louie and Claire won the $100 because Louie made the 3-point shot. Let me analyze the causality here: The condition for winning is: if either Louie makes a 3-point shot or Claire makes a 3-point shot, they win $100. Claire made a 3-point shot at the beginning of the game. Louie made a 3-point shot
